findings.description: u_other_rules_1_0001 bundles three distinct directives: (1) scope statement that the guide governs patient/DTC materials, (2) the patient-vs-HCP terminology rule, and (3) a process obligation to confirm audience in every brief before writing. Only the terminology piece is represented; the scope-of-guide statement and the brief-confirmation workflow requirement are dropped, resulting in an under-split cluster.

The requirement to 'Confirm audience in every brief before writing' is a governance/process obligation (workflow gate before content creation) that is not captured by any candidate rule. It has no facet reflecting when/where the check must occur (at brief stage), leaving a compliance gap.

payload.description: Confirm target audience (patient/DTC vs HCP) in every creative brief before writing begins.

findings.description: Rule is classed as 'assembly' but the source content ('Patient brochure = master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across all LISRAYA materials') describes a document hierarchy/precedence rule, not a physical or layout assembly instruction. Class likely mis-tagged.
